# Framing and positioning vs. modern cheap-talk literature

## Summary
R3 and the editor want the paper repositioned against work that's appeared since the experiment ran (2013). Key references: Backus, Blake & Tadelis (2019, JPE); Tadelis & Zettelmeyer (2015); Ambrus, Chaney & Salitskiy (2018). Needs author-level judgment for positioning; Peyman drafts the more mechanical items.

## Files
- Current intro & lit section: `writeup/hot_towel.tex` (Introduction + §2 Relation to Existing Literature, lines 190–253)
- R3 report: `referee_reports/Hot Towel - AEJ Micro Revision.docx` (R3 §1, §2, §3)
- Combined notes: `revision_notes/Combined_Meeting_Notes.docx` (Theme 4)

## Keep in mind
- The paper's experiment (2013) predates Backus-Blake-Tadelis (2019). R3's main ask: show how this paper's empirical evidence comports with their framework.
- Tadelis & Zettelmeyer (2015) uses the **linkage principle** (not IC; low types would prefer not to reveal). This paper has IC cheap talk. Distinguish clearly.
- The randomized revelation arm maps to Backus et al.'s **garbling device**; Ambrus et al. (2018) is the first use of this design for signaling.
- Don't call a 2007 paper "recent" (p. 2).

## Actionable items

### Coauthor-level positioning
- [ ] Coauthors: write one paragraph explicitly connecting the paper to Backus, Blake & Tadelis (2019, JPE). Outline how this paper's empirical evidence comports with their framework for IC cheap talk + endogenous matching.
- [ ] Coauthors: refine Tadelis & Zettelmeyer (2015) discussion — distinguish linkage principle from IC mechanism.
- [ ] Coauthors: add a paragraph on the garbling device (probabilistic revelation arm) citing Backus et al. (2019) and Ambrus, Chaney & Salitskiy (2018, QE).
- [ ] Coauthors: add connection to costly-signaling / credible-communication in labor markets literature.

### Peyman drafts
- [ ] Draft spelled-out "market" vs. "marketplace" distinction for p. 18 (Roth 2018).
- [ ] Draft citation additions for preferences-for-truthtelling-without-incentives (Kartik 2009; Abeler et al. 2019).
- [ ] Fix introduction claim "lack of incentives makes truth-telling a focal point" — add one of the above citations as evidence.

### Citation cleanup (Peyman)
- [ ] Clean up citation style in last paragraph of p. 2 — currently ambiguous whether cited papers refer to the same labor market.
- [ ] Same ambiguity fix for p. 6 Tadelis & Zettelmeyer reference.
- [ ] p. 2: don't call a 2007 paper "recent."
- [ ] p. 2: move the mid-sentence Horton (2010) reference out of mid-sentence.

## Source
Editor's Letter §2, Additional Comment 4 and §5; R3 §1–§3.

## Owner
Coauthors (positioning paragraphs); Peyman (mechanical fixes).
